subtitle: Ember & Ash Restaurant — Two Horses, One Wreck
location_text: EMBER & ASH RESTAURANT — Acadian / NOLA-adjacent. The night of.

ciphers.text: wrecked SUV at the centre of the diagram — Antonio's chariot, DOA
ciphers.encoding_hint: the chariot card traditionally shows control over duality (two sphinxes/horses); here the wreck is the literal answer — Antonio did NOT keep control. The pillar that was supposed to support the chariot (scaffolding VIP) is what falls. 'TWO HORSES, ONE WRECK' = the duality collapsed.

ciphers.text: sphinx-laid dog beside the wreck — sentinel
ciphers.encoding_hint: the dog ties to the Fool's FAITH (Strength card precursor). In the Chariot tradition, the two sphinxes flank the cart — here, one dog. Half the duality. The other half is the absence: the missing sphinx is Antonio himself.

the BBS network status panel is the chapter's diagnostic surface. SYSOP IS D.O.A. — Antonio has already crashed by the time the player is reading this. The QUENTIN CALL COMPLETE links to the Hierophant card's phone-call binding. SCAFFOLDING CHECK IN PROGRESS foreshadows the structural failure that drops the chariot.

text_lines: THE CREOLE NEGRONI · Local NOLA gin, Campari, sweet vermouth, orange bitters
VENETIAN SAZERAC · Rye whiskey, Peychaud's, absinthe rinse, Italian amaro twist
THE CHARIOT'S FLAME · Mezcal cocktail, chili, lime, flaming garnish
ciphers.encoding_hint: the LIQUID GOLD DISPENSARY menu — three signature cocktails. THE CHARIOT'S FLAME is the one Antonio ordered last. The Negroni and Sazerac trace NOLA/Acadian/Italian lineage of the D'Ambrosio family (Dante = Italian; the Venetian Sazerac links to the family).

text_lines: JIMMY'S SABOTAGE JAMBALAYA — saboteur's portion, slow-cooked, andouille, chicken, spice-packed
TIRAMISU 'SOUTHERN COMFORT' — espresso, mascarpone, ladyfingers, Southern Comfort liqueur
ciphers.encoding_hint: the food menu names two characters: JIMMY (the saboteur — also one of Hierophant's kneelers) and TIRAMISU 'SOUTHERN COMFORT' (the dolce, suggesting the chapter's emotional weather is mock-Southern comfort, ie. the Acadian welcome that bites). The dish names are also UI hooks — clicking each could reveal lore.

ciphers.text: THE LAYOUT [seating map] — Scaffolding VIP / Main Floor / Patio / Bar Section / Kitchen
ciphers.encoding_hint: the seating map is a literal building plan. SCAFFOLDING VIP is the upper section — the section that fails. The wreck happens because the scaffolding VIP collapses (it's literally scaffolding holding people up). 'SCAFFOLDING CHECK IN PROGRESS' in the status panel is the foreman noticing too late.

ciphers.text: BRICKS! tag in the bottom-left — industrial finish call-out
ciphers.encoding_hint: Ember & Ash is a converted brick warehouse — same architectural lineage as Frasier's Cathedral of Rust and Code. 'BRICKS!' is the playful annotation on a designer mood board.

ciphers.text: sysop label reads 'the_charioteer' — Antonio's BBS handle
ciphers.encoding_hint: Antonio operates as 'the_charioteer' on the ember.ash.rest.bbs network. The D.O.A. marker means his BBS process is dead — possibly literally (the wreck) or operationally (he's offline as of now).

ciphers.text: Quentin's voicemail — placed from the St. Jude's booth, three rows back from the rite
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've read AVDI ET TACE on the Hierophant. V → VII adjacency: the Hierophant performs the rite that demands silence; the same booth places the call that summons the Charioteer into motion. The status panel said QUENTIN CALL COMPLETE from the start — clicking now plays the actual voicemail. Antonio committed to the drive because Quentin asked him to. The institution's command (AUDI ET TACE) and the friend's call (the booth phone) are the same gesture in two registers.

ciphers.text: the kneeling boy 'Y' from the Hierophant card is Jimmy — Y of JIMMY'S SABOTAGE JAMBALAYA
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've touched the unnamed Y-acolyte on the Hierophant. The Lovers card's implicit-pair lattice listed MAYA & Y as 'too young; just kneelers' — refusing to commit to the pairing. The Chariot card confirms it indirectly: Y was Jimmy, who became the kitchen's saboteur, whose sabotage IS the wreck. The cards trace the boy from the pew to the burning kitchen via the menu item with his name on it.

ciphers.text: the sphinx-laid dog is Faith — the same dog from John's diner, now beside the wreck
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've touched John's dog on the Fool. The classical Chariot card shows TWO sphinxes — the controlled duality. Modern Mythology renders the duality as ONE dog seen twice across the deck: at the Fool's feet at 3:47 AM, at the wreck site by sunrise. Faith does not appear at the wreck — Faith was already at the wreck before John finished wiping the counter. The card's TWO HORSES, ONE WRECK premise is also TWO STATIONS, ONE DOG.

DORMANT until you've read the Lovers' pair lattice on VI. The Lovers card declined to confirm any pair from its lattice — the chapter's whole gesture is the refusal. The Chariot card has no such patience: the BBS diagnostic prints the pair as a system fact. 'the_charioteer (D.O.A.) ↔ booth_caller (Quentin).' Second entry in the pair lattice flips from implicit to confirmed (John & Faith was the first). The Lovers refuse; the deck consummates anyway, in the registers the Lovers won't speak.

hotspots.notes: the crashed SUV centerpiece — touching it begins the wreck-reconstruction mechanic

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read AVDI ET TACE on the Hierophant. The status panel's QUENTIN CALL COMPLETE line is now interactive — clicking plays the voicemail Quentin left from St. Jude's booth, three rows back from the rite. The same hand that told Maya to be silent placed the call that summoned the Charioteer. V → VII adjacency: the institution's command completes itself in motion.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has touched the unnamed Y-acolyte on the Hierophant card. JIMMY'S SABOTAGE JAMBALAYA on the food menu now has a small annotation: the kneeling boy who couldn't speak in church is the saboteur in the kitchen. Maya & Y is the implicit pair the Lovers card declined to confirm — the Chariot confirms it by showing Y was Jimmy all along.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has touched John's dog on the Fool. The sphinx-laid dog beside the wreck IS Faith. Same dog, two stations of the journey — she leaves the diner counter at 3:47 AM, ends up beside Antonio's wreck. The Fool's lone companion becomes the Chariot's lone sentinel. Classical sphinx-pair is canonically TWO; the deck renders it as one dog twice.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read the Lovers' pair lattice on VI. The sysop line 'the_charioteer (D.O.A.)' gains a second line: '↔ booth_caller (Quentin)' — the QUENTIN & ANTONIO pair from the Lovers lattice is confirmed by the BBS itself. The Lovers card refused to consummate the reading; the Chariot's diagnostic does it anyway, posthumously. Second entry in the pair lattice flips from speculation to confirmed.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has recognized the unknown-number 23:47 line on the Hanged Man card. The BBS status panel gains a third sysop entry: 'natalie@simons_apt (NO ANSWER).' The booth-call chain V → VII → XII is now legible from the wreck site: Quentin called three numbers, Antonio picked up and died, Natalie did not pick up and waits. The deck's call chain closes when the Charioteer card admits its caller was reaching for someone who didn't come.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has spotted THE CHARIOT'S FLAME on Daigle's bar. The drinks menu now has a small annotation next to the signature cocktail: 'last poured: vol5_ch15 — middle stool, Wednesday.' The drink that was Antonio's last is the drink the Devil's middle seat still holds. The menu remembers its own movement. VII → XV chain confirmed from both ends.

easter_eggs: the SYSOP D.O.A. tag is the entire chapter's premise in two letters — Antonio is reading his own diagnostic after the fact, or somebody else is reading it FOR him, depending on how you parse the POV.
QUENTIN CALL COMPLETE in the status panel is the second half of the Hierophant card's BLACK COFFEE NEAT booth scene. Cards V and VII are bound by one phone call.
JIMMY'S SABOTAGE JAMBALAYA is the menu item AND the chapter's true antagonist mechanism — Jimmy (Hierophant's south kneeler) sabotages the scaffolding. Or the chicken.
the SCAFFOLDING VIP section is where the wreck happens. The whole layout is the chapter, read as architecture instead of narrative.
TWO HORSES ONE WRECK is the Tarot Chariot's traditional 'control of duality' inverted — the duality was never controlled. Antonio is half of the team that crashed because the other half didn't exist.
